latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next

latest.title: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail

latest.title: Forests can cool the air below them
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Las hojas convierten luz en azúcar mira cómo

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Iguanas climb trees to escape heat and predators
latest.script_key: this iguana s not eating that s why it s climbing so high see the coconut grooves under its claws cold blooded reptiles need to sunbathe but also watch their step that s why it perches so still would you guess a reptile this calm can drop 30 feet and land feet first

latest.title: Monk's cave lantern reveals a light trick
latest.script_key: watch this lantern twice the monk s light doesn t make shadows because the cave walls are covered in wet smooth rock that acts like a mirror that s why the glow just bounces did your textbook mention that

latest.title: Trees grow stronger roots underwater you missed
latest.script_key: watch this one twice see those zigzag roots hugging the water that s not by accident they re pumping oxygen straight to the trunk like a straw that s why mangroves stand firm in saltwater while other trees drown did you learn this in school

latest.title: Huskies don't shake off just water
latest.script_key: a husky shook itself dry in one second those loose skin folds aren t just for looks they slosh water around like a sponge that s why it can fling off 70 of the water in a single shake ever seen a wet dog pause mid shake that s the lag time in its neck muscles catching up

latest.title: Deer aim their ears like sound radar
latest.script_key: deer can aim each ear toward danger watch the ears first because deer can turn them to sample sound from different directions that quick listening check helps them decide whether to freeze flee or keep feeding one quiet second tells the whole body what to do would you freeze

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next
